name: Working Families Power
subject: Our World Cup
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Power (77 Sands St
body:
| | | **Soccer is the people’s game, Peter.** You see it everywhere — on dusty lots, in city parks, on school fields, even on streets where two backpacks become goalposts. Wherever there’s a ball and room to run, people find a way to play. And when we gather around it? We’re doing more than just watching a sport. We’re sharing joy, building community, and remembering we’re part of something bigger than ourselves. That connection matters — especially now, when so many forces are working overtime to isolate us and distract us from our shared stake in each other's futures. In 2026, the World Cup comes back to the U.S. for the first time since 1994, and millions will take to the streets, parks, and bars to celebrate. **But we’re not letting FIFA and corporate sponsors turn that kind of once-in-a-generation energy into a cash-grab that leaves our communities with nothing.** Instead, we’re showing up the WFP way: by bringing people together, welcoming new neighbors in, and building lasting power for working people long after the final whistle. [**That’s why we’re a part of Our Cup, a new campaign to organize, celebrate, and activate our communities during the 2026 World Cup — and we want you to be a part of it.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMe7YYUEGj1Cabpbe_G8CfVF8TTQ1Du-kgVn3zlzNV6_G56tha-vUb1bM9DB3eeXIIqz-lp-MBzWQqYGQ3hEY070l7PUcmPSbVPOkPjfNh9tPEuinyMYemBVvBuE16RZwsQ1O9QSRZAFkt7-gcIpESWYcvi9pEWrJJ2y-6OZh2fGegLIdCye1AZHVkQgTowLzhZ4SuB6oxqYlz45btGnpPXM/4me/E2IQEsbHQYGDQZM3GqK7EQ/h0/h001.sr-xJXpnz5Cn4HXy2Zrb4I6ExVp19WsRW7M8KP28Eh4) From watch parties to neighborhood gatherings, these will be spaces to celebrate, meet fellow WFP supporters, and organize around what our communities actually need. Because joy, community, and solidarity aren’t a detour from the fight. They’re part of how we sustain it. The games are still months away, but you can get ready now. [**Sign up today to be the first to hear about events near you, ways to plug in, and how we’ll show up during the tournament.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMe7YYUEGj1Cabpbe_G8CfVHwY5EcL2PKXAfNxrfd7m_gkZmxW5y6gz634rFYpH3AvJMeioTiemrAMUD28bu4ehKxQ1fPdo-NMPJzA1JgX3TtK21G2W0wuAILVT22gx0n8xtEJZFIgG6LHJItzpjYJs8lFwYgpOPcZsrSG17eUpIVW06LiRlKlvhyQZvNmeEs1ONcFtkPpTopESRaYWXl0jM/4me/E2IQEsbHQYGDQZM3GqK7EQ/h1/h001.yARtuhskJ_fU9PK7beHBMOGMNWHNq9DP0ztR3wkJ02U) [****](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMe7YYUEGj1Cabpbe_G8CfVGX4731BhAj0qdPnMdub6NX_AAZcikTsp00LoFWNedEiOm02ZyfsdaUzHruwSPhaQfHtUdbZT9OOu_iyXGNwHltHwi00xzE2m4iqpeS00eNh4tER5PfyDQXJleLEExeGyZ9sVmVHunYBfhL8aaO6GTkDOgoef72j2mrAioAqywkhsphPBi7vkGIemTX4kKvrRU/4me/E2IQEsbHQYGDQZM3GqK7EQ/h2/h001.Xw4hiI6Lw3H6qECTUd7K3nvZcLYFT83ee_ECwO3bTIY) **[Sign Up](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMe7YYUEGj1Cabpbe_G8CfVEpn8dlPFetLA2cYrgq_2MmOVOqjj0SqhWdu9BYzjWSE-iI8LzRz02JOPtOS0ouPiKyvlBPbedJMvqSwim6fMfje5KOufrwrtTFAMvquV8Cn97Q5Ig5tgPA7RC-SMcL0rDIbvFOhHmGZsfcXP3winHgZusshKTog3I8HwzwslDJenPCxR6NwtDFqs77WuhGVqo/4me/E2IQEsbHQYGDQZM3GqK7EQ/h3/h001.yuv4NPT5AlEzDUNYM9vP5g8POYf_GhU4bwijsMj4RCo)** **Let’s be real, Peter.** While FIFA and big sponsors rake in billions, it’s working people who make the tournament happen — staffing stadium gates, cooking the food, cleaning the bathrooms, driving the buses, setting up broadcasts, and making and selling the merch that fans take home. It’s also the fans and communities whose love for the game brings every stadium, bar, block, and living room to life. This tournament belongs to all of us, and we’re not letting FIFA or the corporate vultures circling around it pretend otherwise. Last week, FIFA handed Donald Trump their newly-created “Peace Prize” with no clear criteria or transparency on the selection process, even as his administration continues to attack working people on every front. **If that’s the kind of politics FIFA wants tied to this World Cup, we’re bringing a different vision.** We’re bringing this tournament back to where it belongs: in our hands — a multiracial, multigenerational coalition of everyday people. Because our labor and our love make the game possible, and our communities should keep thriving long after the trophy lift. **[Join us, celebrate with us, and help make sure next year’s World Cup lifts up the working people and communities who power it — and strengthens our movement for the long haul.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMe7YYUEGj1Cabpbe_G8CfVE0TiSMJhHQyqBsRoCyHMx2Exw7G3-eJ8jN4csW7fCIR2B2H-MlwFDkWhAhIYjQAOxkSOn8IGiaJd26vhKHSCZHHl9vlswPw31-dXwkrB-FFgqbhgi25zw_20yS4w6q15R27im_Xsp0QFvdMo94YHHlFJol4ZoKA3OGlQWNMQLdZsEWKjX2oxTXssOb_2CqZCc/4me/E2IQEsbHQYGDQZM3GqK7EQ/h4/h001.UH1SHfPr5il_zPxJX24gPrdFp6DTUzfoExujXcpm_8s)** **[Sign Up](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMe7YYUEGj1Cabpbe_G8CfVF3BkhoyZYrDnga-irNe04CIRU_DhgHU8rLdxl33uDobMGGf5jLxsqfnGW7YqEwKZgkgSzwZGICKthK0Bhr5TAIFBw3P5nq_CWbW0Rx569kfgrzxjb11Fgyb13dh1lNvlgzdYAiv3cWUKy-V-nFQu4wRtSMblrutgHeD1gi-Rb92UWMUuKw_rsJjOBvXmcMKJc/4me/E2IQEsbHQYGDQZM3GqK7EQ/h5/h001.76dqGn3TKHLabO_GCGsvcE4TVVKugeDF1LcIFt6LfT8)** In solidarity, Working Families Power Thoughts? clean_body: | | | Soccer is the people’s game, Peter. You see it everywhere — on dusty lots, in city parks, on school fields, even on streets where two backpacks become goalposts. Wherever there’s a ball and room to run, people find a way to play. And when we gather around it? We’re doing more than just watching a sport. We’re sharing joy, building community, and remembering we’re part of something bigger than ourselves. That connection matters — especially now, when so many forces are working overtime to isolate us and distract us from our shared stake in each other's futures. In 2026, the World Cup comes back to the U.S. for the first time since 1994, and millions will take to the streets, parks, and bars to celebrate. But we’re not letting FIFA and corporate sponsors turn that kind of once-in-a-generation energy into a cash-grab that leaves our communities with nothing. Instead, we’re showing up the WFP way: by bringing people together, welcoming new neighbors in, and building lasting power for working people long after the final whistle. From watch parties to neighborhood gatherings, these will be spaces to celebrate, meet fellow WFP supporters, and organize around what our communities actually need. Because joy, community, and solidarity aren’t a detour from the fight. They’re part of how we sustain it. The games are still months away, but you can get ready now. Let’s be real, Peter. While FIFA and big sponsors rake in billions, it’s working people who make the tournament happen — staffing stadium gates, cooking the food, cleaning the bathrooms, driving the buses, setting up broadcasts, and making and selling the merch that fans take home. It’s also the fans and communities whose love for the game brings every stadium, bar, block, and living room to life. This tournament belongs to all of us, and we’re not letting FIFA or the corporate vultures circling around it pretend otherwise. Last week, FIFA handed Donald Trump their newly-created “Peace Prize” with no clear criteria or transparency on the selection process, even as his administration continues to attack working people on every front. If that’s the kind of politics FIFA wants tied to this World Cup, we’re bringing a different vision. We’re bringing this tournament back to where it belongs: in our hands — a multiracial, multigenerational coalition of everyday people. Because our labor and our love make the game possible, and our communities should keep thriving long after the trophy lift. In solidarity, Working Families Power Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. Want to learn more about Working Families Power? Help us build the movement power to win a democracy for the many — not the wealthy and powerful few. Paid for by Working Families Power (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . name: Mike Nelson
subject: I track storms for a living. Boebert's one of them.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bVZS5zbOjqwT4PFyyaN6IV1tbHLeCXWsFDcG--Wo2aVNFF4bOfM-JVQ9XrPqdae0k2US-H4CZpRiK25lHT6UjbKXRlkEqIfmCUCET3galFkITsKG1FzAvr7OWvdWI8ZrI6ZpRbJ7-xLJPPPV74Ah0V5YYgCsea5x6Wo8nwjK1A92Z-xKPod7OG-xZf1QupP3jhge2nEDWDVMGlZwzHz5Ji6JjymDeBI&c=y6X04zZ8tcV2pBT-tugnW8u-ukz74YlIbJ-AXByr0HhpyhGPpCu1ww==&ch=xQ9hu4-CUjqmmIOCopD0eTj7JPuczqkkdw93VU560UPZg1CEEbFxyw==)

Hi Peter, \- I'm Mike Nelson - a longtime news meteorologist and proud Coloradan.
After decades of tracking tornadoes, wildfires, floods and blizzards across our state, I've seen firsthand how timely, reliable weather reporting saves lives - especially in rural areas where help can be hours away.
But recently, Lauren Boebert and her friends in Congress have slashed critical weather and emergency preparedness funding. She backed the "Big, Beautiful Bill," which guts the systems we rely on to predict and prepare for extreme weather. That's not just irresponsible. It's dangerous.
That's why I'm standing with Trisha Calvarese. She actually grew up here. She knows our communities, shows up and listens. And she understands how important it is to protect the resources families count on - weather alerts, a reliable electric grid, emergency services.
We need someone in Congress who puts public safety before partisan soundbites. Someone who takes facts seriously and doesn't gamble with people's lives. That's why I'm asking: [Will you chip in today to help Trisha flip this seat, hold Boebert accountable, and bring real leadership back to Colorado?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019bVZS5zbOjqwT4PFyyaN6IV1tbHLeCXWsFDcG--Wo2aVNFF4bOfM-JVQ9XrPqdae0k2US-H4CZpRiK25lHT6UjbKXRlkEqIfmCUCET3galFkITsKG1FzAvr7OWvdWI8ZrI6ZpRbJ7-xLJPPPV74Ah0V5YYgCsea5x6Wo8nwjK1A92Z-xKPod7OG-xZf1QupP3jhge2nEDWDVMGlZwzHz5GWofMUI-UhX&c=y6X04zZ8tcV2pBT-tugnW8u-ukz74YlIbJ-AXByr0HhpyhGPpCu1ww==&ch=xQ9hu4-CUjqmmIOCopD0eTj7JPuczqkkdw93VU560UPZg1CEEbFxyw==)

name: Redistricting Action
subject: ICYMI: All On The Line is now Redistricting Action
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
Did you catch our latest announcement?
|
---

Peter,
The extreme politicians who are actively manipulating the redistricting process to silence voters and entrench themselves in power have launched an unprecedented, coordinated attack on our democracy.
They’ve forced us into a new era of mid-decade gerrymandering, court battles, and anti-democracy power grabs that require year-round, long-term action.
We wanted to make sure you caught our latest update about the direction of our organization. **All On The Line is now Redistricting Action — a name that captures both who we are and what this moment demands.**
**Read the full announcement below from Attorney General Holder,**[**then add your name to show you’re committed to this new phase of our work fighting for fair maps and a government that actually reflects the will of the people.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121224672/588755123/1419027664?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_RebrandTopperNote1_email_20251213-AOTL-Tier1-RebrandTopperNote1_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20251213-AOTL-Tier1-RebrandTopperNote1_&utm_campaign=RebrandTopperNote1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4MGUwMGI1LTY2ZDgtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=1VdA-t3NvfwnUSZ4RSXhpVBjM_UtaJqpVQfflRv4eV8=&emci=e0891053-9dd7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=d80e00b5-66d8-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=8431045)
\------------ Forwarded Message ------------
From: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
Subject: Announcing: Redistricting Action
Date: Friday, December 12
Peter,
Our democracy is at an inflection point.
President Donald Trump is shamelessly demanding that politicians manipulate maps in order to preserve political power.
And lawmakers in too many state legislatures across the country are falling in line, weakening the voice of the people by passing egregious gerrymanders.
Meanwhile, anti-democracy groups are assaulting voting rights on multiple fronts — from challenging the Voting Rights Act in court to openly undermining an accurate count in the next census.
Peter, these are not separate fights. They’re all connected. And they represent our new reality. We’re in an era of perpetual redistricting and ever-increasing attacks on the American people’s freedom to vote.
**That’s why we’re announcing that All On The Line is becoming Redistricting Action — a name that captures both who we are and what this moment demands. Because redistricting isn’t just a policy fight — it’s an ongoing struggle for a representative democracy.**
[**This change is about rising to the challenges we face with the urgency they require — taking action together to build a representative democracy. Add your name and let me know I can count on you to do your part in the critical mission ahead.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121224673/588755124/1419027664?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_RebrandTopperNote1_email_20251213-AOTL-Tier1-RebrandTopperNote1_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20251213-AOTL-Tier1-RebrandTopperNote1_&utm_campaign=RebrandTopperNote1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4MGUwMGI1LTY2ZDgtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=1VdA-t3NvfwnUSZ4RSXhpVBjM_UtaJqpVQfflRv4eV8=&emci=e0891053-9dd7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=d80e00b5-66d8-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=8431045)
In February 2019 when President Barack Obama and I launched the All On The Line campaign, we wanted to design a nationwide grassroots movement for fair maps and fair representation.
From the start, that movement has been about empowering local organizers to become redistricting experts by offering free training and providing the resources activists need to make a difference in their communities.
And it worked.
Volunteers across the country have testified at hearings, protested against unfair maps, and organized their neighbors to demand accountability from those in power.
**Together, we’ve built a people-powered movement that has changed the conversation on redistricting and made a real impact in the fight for fair representation.**
But since the last redistricting cycle and especially this year, we’ve entered a new era of mid-decade gerrymandering, court battles, and anti-democracy power grabs. The reality is, if we want a government that actually reflects the people, we need fair maps — and that requires staying engaged, organized, and ready to fight.
Redistricting Action is here to do that work. Peter, we were built to meet that challenge. But we need partners, allies, voters, and supporters like you to stay in the fight with us.
[**If you believe it’s time for fair districts and a truly representative democracy, please share your name today to kick off this new, urgent phase of our work.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121224674/588755125/1419027664?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_RebrandTopperNote1_email_20251213-AOTL-Tier1-RebrandTopperNote1_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20251213-AOTL-Tier1-RebrandTopperNote1_&utm_campaign=RebrandTopperNote1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4MGUwMGI1LTY2ZDgtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=1VdA-t3NvfwnUSZ4RSXhpVBjM_UtaJqpVQfflRv4eV8=&emci=e0891053-9dd7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=d80e00b5-66d8-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=8431045)
Sincerely,
Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States

Peter, I won’t waste your time: I’m reaching out because as 2025 comes to a close, our final FEC deadline is rapidly approaching. **This is our last major test before the midterm election year begins. All eyes will be on our fundraising report -- our supporters, opponents, and the national GOP will be combing over our performance.** This race is in a dramatically different place than the beginning of the year:
* Proposition 50 passed in California, and added tens of thousands of new voters to our district. These are folks that have never seen my name on a ballot, and we’re racing to reach them through our voter outreach efforts.

* My Republican opponent wants to flip this seat back to red and stick to the status quo in Washington -- an agenda that has wreaked havoc on working families who are struggling to make ends meet. He also boasts an endorsement from Mike Garcia, the GOP incumbent we defeated in 2024.

**[I’m working hard in Washington to deliver real results back to our communities, and I’ll be honest, it doesn’t leave much time to make sure we hit our key deadlines. clean_body: | = Peter, I won’t waste your time: I’m reaching out because as 2025 comes to a close, our final FEC deadline is rapidly approaching. This is our last major test before the midterm election year begins. All eyes will be on our fundraising report -- our supporters, opponents, and the national GOP will be combing over our performance. This race is in a dramatically different place than the beginning of the year: Proposition 50 passed in California, and added tens of thousands of new voters to our district. These are folks that have never seen my name on a ballot, and we’re racing to reach them through our voter outreach efforts. My Republican opponent wants to flip this seat back to red and stick to the status quo in Washington -- an agenda that has wreaked havoc on working families who are struggling to make ends meet. | | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001l3rOKd70iH3iZhCNUZbClQRJjsdHrMyKBD9IJnh5o2a1i5HfNfUW0Qq6zPPmULsJLwn3vFiUWZ8c02GRUxNqrmR41bbDNbpCQx11i76CvqMROA1UuGNe6WKHb1pa0cn7maG8ruFYL0ZMCVW3QospT5xyb4qekXFDMfbFUiNGsu6njD5alF_NVHT3OHs5QaLqCSvAUv4PszxCf3wmMzjXv24xdrHe_tvP&c=tlQ9u6RcvKrA9eE8H01eRA72KDsr6tUfRI58gMfCxFjN38AhyxoB2w==&ch=RfUtYF7bHLVrhdIoHde5gUQUNGiPSVrEMhjt2OWwrhqDKGtAFf9OGA==)

Hi Peter, \- I'm Mike Nelson - a longtime news meteorologist and proud Coloradan.
After decades of tracking tornadoes, wildfires, floods and blizzards across our state, I've seen firsthand how timely, reliable weather reporting saves lives - especially in rural areas where help can be hours away.
But recently, Lauren Boebert and her friends in Congress have slashed critical weather and emergency preparedness funding. She backed the "Big, Beautiful Bill," which guts the systems we rely on to predict and prepare for extreme weather. That's not just irresponsible. It's dangerous.
That's why I'm standing with Trisha Calvarese. She actually grew up here. She knows our communities, shows up and listens. And she understands how important it is to protect the resources families count on - weather alerts, a reliable electric grid, emergency services.
We need someone in Congress who puts public safety before partisan soundbites. Someone who takes facts seriously and doesn't gamble with people's lives. That's why I'm asking: [Will you chip in today to help Trisha flip this seat, hold Boebert accountable, and bring real leadership back to Colorado?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001l3rOKd70iH3iZhCNUZbClQRJjsdHrMyKBD9IJnh5o2a1i5HfNfUW0Qq6zPPmULsJLwn3vFiUWZ8c02GRUxNqrmR41bbDNbpCQx11i76CvqMROA1UuGNe6WKHb1pa0cn7maG8ruFYL0ZMCVW3QospT5xyb4qekXFDMfbFUiNGsu6njD5alF_NVHT3OHs5QaLqCSvAUv4PszxCf3wmMzjXvx-U9Dal0Gxl&c=tlQ9u6RcvKrA9eE8H01eRA72KDsr6tUfRI58gMfCxFjN38AhyxoB2w==&ch=RfUtYF7bHLVrhdIoHde5gUQUNGiPSVrEMhjt2OWwrhqDKGtAFf9OGA==)

name: Jack Schlossberg
subject: Not just here to stir the pot.
body:
Peter, I had a feeling my social media presence would ruffle some feathers... but there's a method here. And I had the honor of talking about it on MSNBC the other night.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/613936715357375A6B3664466A4C506A6E68366A7338363159565A3833396F697A4E755A726331486A524F42313861594E305A35502F47376765535045587347/editor_images/Jack%20MSNBC.gif]
In today's media landscape, you have to cut through the noise to make an impact... You know I can have fun online, but I'm serious about New Yorkers having leadership they can count on.
America is at a dangerous crossroads right now, and it's not the time to sit back and stay quiet to keep people comfortable. It's time to call out what's going on and actually do something to change our course.
NY-12 needs a congressperson who will meet this moment. Peter, I will be that leader. If you're with me, can I count on you to split a donation between Real Texas and my campaign now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112025_3]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jack Schlossberg and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112025_3&amount=25]
If we continue on Trump's path, we face a country devoid of what makes us who we are: the land of opportunity, prosperity, and freedom.
I've used my platform to take on the Trump administration. time and time again I'm not afraid to call out injustice when I see it. And NAME, I'm not just here to stir the pot. I'm uniquely positioned to stand up for you and have a real impact.
In order to get to Congress, we need to build a winning team of grassroots supporters like YOU.
I promise it's not about how much you give, it’s about having you on our side!!
Split a donation between Real Texas and my campaign to show your support! Even just $5 today makes an impact on the size and the strength of our movement. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112025_3]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jack Schlossberg and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=112025_3&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=112025_3&amount=25]
Thanks for believing in me and this campaign. My team and I can’t wait to share more about what we’ve been working on! Stay tuned 👀
Talk soon,
Jack

* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=162%2E124072%2ESPcrGO)**

Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.

name: James Talarico for Texas
subject: If we fail…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
We can't afford to take anything for granted. This race looks entirely different from a week ago.
And the truth is, that makes the stakes even higher for this month’s FEC deadline. It’s our chance to prove our movement has what it takes to flip Texas and the Senate majority.
But there’s also the risk that we falter. Plenty of people are hoping we fail — no one more than the corporate and billionaire donors who profit from our corrupt status quo.
They want to keep us divided. They want you to feel like nothing can change.
But it’s not true. If we come together, we can take back power for ourselves. But it requires each of us to step up and play a role in the fight.
We’re sorry to keep asking, but with all eyes on our campaign, this FEC deadline is a critical moment to show our strength.
[**Please make a contribution of whatever amount you can afford to James Talarico’s campaign for the U.S. Senate. It will send a powerful message that we are not to be underestimated.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251213-1-ak&t=1&akid=316%2E1347274%2E8JRywb)**
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._
| | **[Donate $5](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/506?c0=jt-em-launch.3s2pDp&refcode=em251213-1-ak&t=20&akid=316%2E1347274%2E8JRywb)**

---
****

Team Talarico

Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761

James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=28&akid=316%2E1347274%2E8JRywb).

Team Talarico Paid for by Talarico for Texas Talarico for Texas PO Box 14508 Austin, TX 78761

name: Kevin Kijewski for Michigan Attorney General
subject: 🇺🇸 Kevin Kijewski for AG - December Update 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kevin Kijewski for Michigan, 115 W Allegan St, Ste 900, Lansing, MI 48933_
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/kevinformi/kevin-kijewski-for-ag-december-update-4741137?e=4b10910eed)
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=2dbca2a870&e=4b10910eed)
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=16dfe50264&e=4b10910eed)
| | | | | |
# December Update from Kevin
---
| | Dear Friend and Patriot,
Merry Christmas! We have been very busy this past week traveling across the nation and the state on our pathway to reclaim Michigan as your next Michigan Attorney General in 2026.
What a great December it's been for our campaign to reclaim the Michigan Attorney General's office and make Michigan safe, normal, and red again in 2026! [We kicked off last week with an incredible trip in Washington, DC](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=9367712c6e&e=4b10910eed), where I was invited to meet at the White House with President Trump's team and America First leaders, discussing issues and strategies to restore integrity to the Michigan AG’s office - strategies to defend parental rights, end lawfare, ensure election integrity, protect our workers, and restore the rule of law.
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=4d4b19976c&e=4b10910eed)
| | | | | | | | | | | |
I was also the only Michigan AG candidate invited by the [Republican Attorneys General Association (RAGA)](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=b7a2cc4628&e=4b10910eed) to attend their winter meeting in DC, spending valuable time with over 15 elected Republican AGs over several days who unanimously agreed: Michigan is a top pickup opportunity in 2026, and they are fully committed to helping us flip the seat red. Special thanks to friends like [West Virginia's JB McCuskey](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=3e58935f74&e=4b10910eed), former [Kentucky AG (and future U.S. Senator) Daniel Cameron](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=387f8d1d5b&e=4b10910eed), and [Pennsylvania AG Dave Sunday](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=72a89aba76&e=4b10910eed) for their encouragement, advice, and insights. We also met with members of Congress and major national organizations that are willing to join us in our fight to restore integrity to the AG’s office.
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=ddfe5493d0&e=4b10910eed)
| [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=885a3f6ec8&e=4b10910eed)
| [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=8d34fbdb99&e=4b10910eed)
| |

**Endorsement news:**
I’m pleased to announce that the following patriots have endorsed our campaign for AG, recognizing our commitment to protecting Michigan families and restoring integrity to Lansing. They have joined our coalition, lending their voice to our mission of making Michigan great again for all residents, not just some. These endorsements from key GOP party officials matter. They reflect that more people across the state see the urgency of this race and want real leadership.
* Asuka Barden, Charlevoix GOP County Chair
* Susan Fournier, Mecosta GOP County Chair
* Bruce Rendon, Missaaukee County

---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=d5d0acc63c&e=4b10910eed)
| |

Just before going to Washington, I had the honor of [speaking at the inaugural Huron County GOP Lincoln Dinner](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=d7d7da6aaf&e=4b10910eed) – nearly 200 patriots gathered with electric energy as we shared the vision to prosecute violent crime, protect Second Amendment rights, and end government overreach.
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=201cb8b3f4&e=4b10910eed)
| [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=f63226fb8a&e=4b10910eed)
| |

After arriving back in Michigan, I drove straight from the airport to the [11th District GOP Annual Christmas Party in Oakland County](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=d45b52770e&e=4b10910eed) – celebrating faith, family, and freedom with enthusiastic Republicans ready to turn the AG seat red. It was great to speak to a very encouraging and great crowd in my home congressional district.
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=8394943466&e=4b10910eed)
| [|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=5ba7328921&e=4b10910eed)
| |

Last but certainly not least, [President Trump’s Department of Justice took decisive action on election integrity](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=0f7a7afce9&e=4b10910eed), now suing 18 states (including Michigan) for refusing to comply with federal laws requiring clean voter rolls and transparency. Secretary of State Jocelyn Benson and AG Dana Nessel are defying crystal-clear federal statutes: Michiganders deserve secure elections where every legal vote counts. Instead of fighting costly lawsuits on the taxpayers' dime, Benson and Nessel should comply with the law, release the data, and clean the rolls. As your next Attorney General, I'll prioritize election integrity - no partisan games, full transparency, and prosecution of any fraud.
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=b16e4e9968&e=4b10910eed)
| | As we close out 2025, I'm deeply grateful for patriots like you standing with us. Together, we'll restore trust, safety, and freedom in Michigan!
I’m committed to keeping you informed and welcome your feedback. As Michigan's next Attorney General, I’ll fight to uphold the rule of law, prosecute dangerous criminals, protect our Second Amendment rights, stop political prosecutions, and defend the hardworking people of this state from government overreach. [To win against the radical left's agenda, please contribute today and join the movement to take Michigan back!](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=0cd2af9287&e=4b10910eed)
Very truly yours,Kevin Kijewski
Candidate for Michigan Attorney General
---
[|
---](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=8e0b70c4c1&e=4b10910eed)
| | | | | [](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=9b70108422&e=4b10910eed)
---

[|

[|

name: Paul Chitwood
subject: The mission continues — and today we begin
body:

Dear Friend,

As we complete the Week of Prayer and move into this new season of giving, I want to thank you for standing with us. Your prayers, your heart for the nations, and your faithfulness mean more than you know.

Today, we officially launch our **2025 Lottie Moon Christmas Offering campaign** --a yearly effort that has fueled the spread of the gospel for generations.

This offering is not just a Southern Baptist tradition.
It is the backbone of sending IMB missionaries.
It is the reason the unreached hear the gospel.
It is how churches are planted where none existed before.
And it is how people who have never heard the name of Jesus finally hear it for the very first time.

**59% of the world's population has no meaningful access to the gospel.** Many live in places where no Christian witness exists--no neighbor, no pastor, no church. But thanks to your generosity, that's changing--one relationship at a time.
One of our field teams recently shared:

"A team of young adults from Georgia arrived in Central Asia eager to share the gospel, only to learn that in this culture, the good news must first be seen before it is spoken. As they hiked, laughed, and shared meals with local college students, trust slowly formed -- and when it did, doors opened. One woman pulled a team member aside in an old church and asked to hear the whole story of Jesus; another student told a teammate, after days of friendship, that he was finally ready to talk about faith. Lives were changed simply because these young believers were present, patient, and intentional."

This is why we go.
This is why we send.
**[This is why your gift to the Lottie Moon Christmas Offering matters.](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/VGcHmqLMRbRJAqSgmILZqsAoiAgcjgwqErunwoUH2ZnWpAuQaTwPyxN0VZTI3ZNulTs-h2HvZG3PsKA-HGt8P5Fi-xz5LTeewCowoD7hGi2Sf2oGXBoyyBsOEUSTPoLZbgYrZHJ2zXa_crGdNuWTF9gsy8J-Cf_4vnVbykpNYiblWuhtnS_NTSWDUWNeBUh6kum5l2KBDy0gOS7xnXcGQ4YTTNNLB-4PpgBAU5nDc-9fH8MwYtjPFQ15dRbsBTEgeFquzw2mG4EnQ9llCQGVQFmKxh1V7qHurJ00mLweDdgwrA8tOJwl9ejHzjcCQeGaEaPVuHbjx6gB9c72JB0C46GOlmlGHXfsscOIIdQQ1Y7xFWEGJ-IzwCpVOyaQdj7PeDAXFAbDeAiRs98pSB_gR5usedNjL71ErCGmaNEpftsTAHEH7jxsttk1mpC-l-DAee5y9Gv-IpQ3Bw0o4XNgf1JmiT6vbxCx7xt0qoEm52AjRWhs703-6v-sYp6AYyu2AKfMoao8rc8XU8TyH80XJhjNRChHueeeCC-DjQ1GQ9vcngI1FQ8ud8OVvAj9W2ETxQM4am5yijr1ZIel_wv4FwZ8WgwG8T6ztTkCDUHEBsfX4Ge8gXHnDdEH85pmVGxBI1G_sdajomi0JizRoQaVcRhMNaHQDJg4ZjG6HEaEePpBA_fDK_wSID28tMYnbx6aF5Mq9PhmgKEGHN77-dGwCG3c3CR8r74KZ9_uUdTwnj1KVZEo9Nrf/uHZiLbdeDIpdMtNivefGJegENnFhvL4i/15)**

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: When AI systems are trained on biased data, they produce biased outcomes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
AI is shaping who gets a house, a job, a loan, or lifesaving medical care.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251213-5706-footer&t=1&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t)
Peter,
Artificial intelligence is now a projected $244 billion industry, and it’s already embedded in everything from hiring software to mortgage approvals. It’s shaping who gets a house, a job, a loan, or lifesaving medical care — often without anyone knowing how the decision was made.
**And when AI systems are trained on biased data, they produce biased outcomes. It’s increasingly happening in the algorithms that quietly govern our lives.**
That’s why I recently introduced the _**AI Civil Rights Act**_ in the House of Representatives, the most comprehensive effort yet to ensure that the technologies of the future are built on fairness, transparency, and accountability — not the injustices of the past.
**Here’s what the bill does:**
* Prevents companies from using biased or discriminatory AI in critical decisions that affect peoples’ lives

* Requires testing before and after deployment so potential harms are caught and fixed, not ignored

* Demands transparency around complex algorithms so that people know how decisions about them are made

These are common-sense solutions that will make sure AI serves people instead of discriminating against them. **But Big Tech is already lobbying hard against new guardrails** , because they want a world where their profits are protected and working people shoulder all the risk.
[**That’s why I’m asking you to help me build the momentum we need to beat them by chipping in $3, or anything you can, to support my campaign.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251213-1.hF66Na&refcode=em20251213-5706&amount=3&t=2&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251213-1.hF66Na&amount=3&refcode=em20251213-5706&t=21&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t)**
I don’t take corporate PAC money, which means grassroots support is the only reason I can lead fights like this. Your donations fuel my ability to push back against corporate lobbyists and build the broad coalition needed to pass this bill.
So if you can, please [**chip in today**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251213-1.hF66Na&refcode=em20251213-5706&amount=3&t=22&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t) and help me keep up the fight to build a government that puts working people first.
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20251213-5706-footer&t=23&refcode2=5706_420207_a3hh2t&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=24&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=5706%2E420207%2Ea3hh2t)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: Voter Action Project Research Team
subject: The MAGA push for one-day voting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
You are invited to join our one-click issue response poll.
For data integrity purposes, please do not complete this survey if your email address is not dpwillis67@gmail.com .
Team: Cleta Mitchell -- a Trump lawyer, anti-voter conspiracy theorist, and vote suppressor -- and other activists from a top anti-voting group with close ties to the White House met recently to discuss plans to push for severely limiting early and mail voting .
Those potential cutbacks, which echo what President Donald Trump has called for in speeches and executive orders, would radically curtail access to the ballot, taking the U.S. back decades to the era of one-day elections . Cleta Mitchell reported to fellow activists afterwards that the group wants nearly all voting to take place in person, on Election Day.
Their plan would dramatically limit in-person early voting, which has helped make it easier for voters who work long hours or lack flexibility in their schedule to cast a ballot. Mail-in voting also would be curtailed under their plan, and the plan would even bar local election officials from printing additional ballots if they run out on Election Day.
We're looking to see how our supporters feel about this effort. Please take our quick survey:
Team: Do you agree with Trump and Mitchell that we should go back to the era of one-day elections?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=121325_2yes&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=121325_2no&amount=15]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121325_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121325_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=idYRJPQfQFuv31VIU%2BgY6yF%2F3r2iBJzNnm15O6h3u4m1bs%2BbhmUJ2hejG01JbWCtEFTPEm%2BEz%2BRlo0gwOO9WGn7CjaUm44RAc%2BSXmDzpiWWCEvavtZWXajSWso5nIpLyOMa3l%2FRGtcgLnm4h259wYfvGHfqwvGCvOAMFeCWRopoXYPu3u5VqogpZzT6%2Fv7qEUsFaHnZRnCH4Au8CsBWswwF%2BgCep992cqeteuhDRI0DClLXLLqK3X8Xfe9ng0HrwvArrvzjYgcheoThyz8s1LCDGZxeTzuJ7Gma2SMylaeb8JoB0iZ9fULGJaZWydZD2IbJ5n80BMOLlU57QS7B9uERJZTQeCNieP4Ih8cCYX62pWslqC2bC9Nur5qvYI8%2FtvA%2FpT0OCWJaBKZYn6zCIsn1xsnazSEEemel%2BDcByK%2BM%3D .

| Update 13 December 2025
---
| |
---
| Team,
President Donald J. Trump, Speaker Mike Johnson, Leader John Thune and the rest of us are working hard to reverse the radical leftist Biden agenda and restore freedom to all Americans. By the grace of God, we have an incredible team and we are helping people of all backgrounds solve problems all over the 5th district. We have returned close to 6.5 million dollars owed to constituents, regardless of race, party, religion, or creed, back to their pockets.
As we continue to fight for you in Washington D.C., please continue to send us your prayers and advice. Affordability, healthcare, law and order, and more will be on the ballot in 2026.
We can win the midterms but we must maximize efforts on turnout and messaging. We need all hands on deck. If you voted for me I love you. If you did not vote for me I love you. It is not about me, it is about saving our country.
To give you some ammo or facts to discuss with others, here are some of our accomplishments we have achieved as a team so far in less than one year:

More Bad Leadership in the 5th

The childish pattern of dysfunctional, and ego-driven behavior out of the 5th committee continued this morning at the 5th District GOP quarterly meeting. I listened to the recording. Instead of discussing how we will unite and defeat the democrats and win the midterms, the chair tried rewriting history by trying to convince people my team and I didn't help the ticket in November. Everyone in the district is quite certain no one out worked us. He also continued disparaging fellow Republicans.
That is why myself and many others have no faith in the 5th District GOP chairman's ability to lead anything. If he hates the position so much, he should resign. body:
There’s something I need to tell you...

| | | | [](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-wkb61N7RKgkY-NSw)Hey, Peter. It’s Marsha.
I know it’s late, and you’re probably getting settled for the night, but I need to tell you something that can’t wait until the morning.
I’m just **$231** short of today's fundraising goal. I know this doesn’t seem like a lot. But trust me when I say every single dollar counts when it comes to defeating the Democrats and protecting our GOP Majority.
If we fall short of our goal tonight, it will set the stage for a complete Democrat takeover. Every GOP victory in the past few weeks will be gone overnight!
[](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)[This is why failure is not an option here, Peter. I’m calling on every patriot reading this email to chip in $4. Nothing more. Nothing less. Just $4.](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-7vjQw)[Chip In $4
](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-UX0HSYDJW6A)If everyone reading this email contributes just $4 or more, we’ll EASILY surpass our fundraising goal and have the resources necessary to stop the Democratic takeover.
_If not, we’re doomed..._[ Chip In $4
](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn._XP0Jnp5g)God Bless you, and have a good night,
Marsha Blackburn| | |

body:
Take a look at this photo of my grandmother Polly Noonan and my mother Polly Rutnik. |
I want to share something deeply personal with you. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226131/588759924/-31663778?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.13_Women-Who-Raised-Me_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Yzc1YTY0Ny03M2Q4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YKKLk-FHGcD3SJqH3zfU2N9zRMVrlYbamONcQMc8Q28=) Take a look at this photo — that’s my grandmother Polly Noonan and my mother Polly Rutnik. They’re the reason I ever stepped into politics. My grandmother was a secretary in Albany, but she didn’t stay in the background. At a time when most women weren’t involved in politics, she became a force — organizing “Polly’s Girls.” They hosted rallies, circulated petitions, threw fundraisers and knocked on doors — showing women they belonged in public life. My mom was one of the only women in her law school class — and even took the Bar Exam three days before giving birth to me. She built a career in a field that often shut women out. Because of them, I grew up believing that my voice mattered. I spent weekends as a kid stuffing envelopes and slapping bumper stickers on cars — learning that civic participation is a serious responsibility. **My Grandma Polly and my mom are the reason I’m still fighting now. Far-right extremists want to silence women and set us back decades. I won’t back down — and I hope you won’t either.** Today, far-right extremists are doing everything they can to silence women, control our bodies and drag our country backward. They want to undo decades of hard-fought progress. And I refuse to let them erase the legacies of women like Polly — not on my watch. That’s exactly why I founded Off the Sidelines — and why I’m still in this fight today as a U.S. Senator and as Chair of the DSCC. Because democracy isn’t a spectator sport. And neither is equality. So here’s my question for you: **_[Will you help Off the Sidelines elect a new wave of leaders in the midterms and defeat the MAGA extremists who are trying to send us back to the past? body:
They’re furious — and that means we’re winning.
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226162/588760042/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)
Hi team, Robert Garcia here.
You may have seen the headline.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226163/588760043/789508428?refcode=251213_RG_dismay-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)
Let me tell you: I love it.
Because when the work I’m doing is “to GOP’s dismay,” it means we’re getting closer to the truth. It means we’re holding bullies accountable. And it means Donald Trump and his allies are feeling the pressure.
**[Before I go any further, I need to ask — will you donate $10 to help us keep pushing this investigation forward and protect our Democratic House majority?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226164/588760044/789508428?refcode=251213_RG_dismay-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226165/588760045/-309737922?refcode=251213_RG_dismay-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)
Here’s what really happened: My team and I combed through more than 20,000 documents — page by page, hour by hour — because the American people deserve answers. When we uncovered emails suggesting Trump knew far more about Epstein’s abuse than he let on, I didn’t hesitate. I released them.
**And the GOP lost it.** They attacked me personally, called me names, and accused me of “sensationalizing” the truth.
I was unfazed because I know why they’re so angry:
_Because the strategy is working.
Because sunlight is working.
Because accountability is working. _
**And because when a bully punches, I punch back — harder.**
**This is our plan: Expose the truth. Force transparency. Build public pressure. Make it impossible for the GOP to bury the facts. That’s how we win this fight — and many others.**
But I cannot do this alone.
**[Will you make a donation today to help me continue this work and defend our democracy?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226166/588760046/789508428?refcode=251213_RG_dismay-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226167/588760047/-309737922?refcode=251213_RG_dismay-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)
Thank you for standing with me. I promise you this: I will never back down.
Robert Garcia
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121226168/588760048/-309737922?refcode=251213_RG_dismay-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODVhYmY2NS03NGQ4LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mi2RVuDTG0LVBfu5n07F2KO7_1Db1NLElxOC93FYAcQ=)

PolitiBrawl contributor and brave independent reporter Richie McGinniss documented the horrific riots during the summer of 2020 on the ground as a reporter armed only with a phone to record what was happening in real time.
Here is an excerpt from his new book _**[RIOT DIET: One Man's Radical Ride Through America in Chaos](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
Are you Christmas shopping for anyone who called the 2020 BLM riots "Peaceful Protests?" **[This is the perfect book to gift them this Christmas...](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
### _Pick up the book today:_
# RIOT DIET: One Man's Radical Ride Through America in Chaos
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

* * *
# LORD OF THE CHAZ
**By Richie McGinniss**
## **June 11, 2020: Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington**
"This is our island. It's a good island. Until the grown ups come to fetch us we'll have some fun."
--Ralph, _Lord of the Flies_
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Thanks to the pandemic, Shelby, Jorge, and I enjoyed an empty flight, but now we were packed in the back of our rideshare from the airport to Capitol Hill. I marveled at the sight of Seattle's modern cityscape for the first time. I narrated while zooming my phone camera into the fog beyond the Seahawks' stadium, "Well, folks, somewhere over there, there's a new country. And we're gonna see it."
After a week of protests and multiple riots throughout Seattle's Capitol Hill, on Friday, June 5, Mayor Jenny Durkan promised a 30-day ban on the use of chemical irritants, pending an internal review. "This review should better emphasize de-escalation techniques." What ensued for the next two days was anything but "de-escalation" and was instead a continuation of the same shit show--rioters threw bricks and bottles, and shot fireworks at police. By Sunday, June 7, under a barrage of improvised projectiles, cops desperately discharged chlorine tear gas, the chemical irritant that the city's politicians pledged not to use.
In the days before our trip, I watched the escalation of progressive violence from a computer screen in D.C. It concluded with a police evacuation as cops hastily loaded onto armored vehicles and drove away from Seattle's East Precinct. Hundreds of jubilant activists and agitators filled the void behind them. A trumpet man ushered in the occupying army of demonstrators. The boarded-up front of the station endured over a week of anti-police protests-turned-riots before they finally capitulated to the mob. As night fell on Monday, the BLM demonstrators took control of the front of the police HQ on the top of the hill. A rapper named Raz distributed a trunk-full of AR-15s. By sunrise the next morning, they set up roadblocks and established checkpoints, expanding their inchoate dominion to a five-block area in the heart of the city. The ousters triumphantly named their new fiefdom the Capitol Hill Autonomous Zone [CHAZ].
On our ride to CHAZ beneath the slanting sun, we drove past Seattle's Pioneer District, where the city's founders had settled in 1852. At the center of the district rose the Smith Tower, which was the tallest building west of the Mississippi River at the time of its completion in 1914. The 34-story building was one of the oldest monuments to corporate prospecting in Seattle. New York typewriter and firearms tycoon Lyman Smith financed the neoclassical structure, hence the name. In the background of my shot, the larger skyscrapers that now dwarf Smith Tower disappeared into the misty low clouds--today's tech companies elevated above the industrial revolutionaries of the past. Somewhere beneath the foggy skyline was a post-modern experiment without sanctioned businesses, or police, or posing politicians.
At the entrance of the CHAZ, a colonial-looking sign painted white and cut in the shape of a house, said in neatly stenciled letters, "You Are Now Entering Free Cap Hill." _Can 't be worse than that open-air drug market in San Juan_, I thought. An amalgamation of city planters, road construction dividers, metal barricades, and plywood created a de facto wall marking the border. The individuals who tended the checkpoint wore all black and carried walkie-talkies, but they appeared more interested in socializing than security.
The first thing we recorded as we stepped inside was a giant "Black Lives Matter" mural that spanned the width of East Pine Street, stretching eastward two blocks up the hill to the police precinct.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
At the intersection of East Pine Street and 11th Avenue, next to the "R" in the aforementioned BLM mural, stood a piece of plywood that read "Defund/Not Decaf/Coffee Tea Cocoa." Directly in the center of the intersection, a set of couches was arranged in a circle. I narrated from behind the camera, adding context for the viewers, "We got a lounge zone over here?" Two signs drawn on cardboard, propped up by art easels with sharpie-drawn letters, said "Conversation Cafe/Let's Talk About Antifascism." The patrons of the cafe sat forward in their relaxed furniture, seemingly engaged in some intense political discussion--passing the conch. All sorts of graffiti covered the ground with messages like "FUCK THE POLICE" written in elegantly scripted periwinkle letters.
When I summited the hill in front of the precinct, I hit record on my phone, cracked a spiked seltzer, and aimed the camera at a man dressed in full knight's armor. He lowered the metal visor over his eyes, clanking it shut with a hard slap. I exclaimed from behind the camera, "White Claws, no laws!" but got no response.
We walked a block beyond the abandoned police precinct and arrived at the intersection of Pine and 13th Avenue, where someone had parked an old Chevy Suburban horizontally across the street. A variety of barriers stacked alongside the vehicle reinforced the checkpoint that marked the easterly reaches of the dominion. Three centurions manned the roadblock, smoking cigarettes and conversing amongst themselves. One man with a bright red beard and scraggly red hair flowing from a blue construction helmet sat on top of the '90s-era truck.
I pulled out my phone and hit record, aiming at the ground at first to avoid upsetting the security detail. The guy on the roof was in the middle of a spirited diatribe about how to properly build a shield from PVC pipe, pool noodles, and duct tape. He had the panache of a hardcore LARPer paired with the garrulous bravado of your high school's most annoying theater kid. "If the Zulu shields, which were just wood and leather, could stop a rifle round, duct tape can stop a rifle round. You just need enough of it!"
I injected myself into the conversation. "Yeah, the Zulus did beat the Brits that one time."
He brought a cigarette up to his mouth, raised the lighter, but then gestured with the cigarette instead of lighting it. "It was the only time that a technologically inferior group managed to whoop the ever-living shit out of the British!"
I chuckled. Now that we had him talking, Shelby pivoted to a more relevant topic: "Do you think that the police--they came today, right?"
* * *
### _Pick up the book today:_
# RIOT DIET: One Man's Radical Ride Through America in Chaos
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Get The Book](https://substack.com/redirect/fb987820-9338-45f1-bee9-2ad12c5c5616?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
He waved the unlit cigarette in her direction. "You know they might come back. They might be hiding in some bunker, gathering resources, waiting for one big-ass push against us. And I'd rather, when they show up with their goons with riot shields, we have our own, going--" He pulled both his hands up as if he held an imaginary shield in a defensive position with the cigarette protruding from his fist and yelled, "Ours look cooler!"
Shelby laughed nervously and sputtered, "Yeah."
The guard took the lighter and cigarette up to his mouth, but lowered them again. Now he aimed the cig at me. "You seem like you're, you know, of some significance!"
I laughed from behind the camera, and Shelby chimed in, always eager to chirp me when given the opportunity. "Don't give him a big ego."
We all laughed, and the guard added gregariously, "Hey, we're all important. We're all just doing our part." He finally lit his cigarette.
Shelby used the silence to shift the conversation again. "How long have you been out here for?"
The didactic defender took a long huff on the cigarette before he replied. "I just got here today. I was at the [May] 30th protest." He coughed into his sleeve before he finished the story. "Got my ass beat by police. Healed up. Came back out!" He smoked the cigarette satisfactorily.
Shelby pressed. "It seems like there's no police out here tonight."
Exhaling smoke, the guard growled, "Yeah, but you got the fuckin' alt-right retards threatenin', posturin', and just, like, all right, come out here. I am more Aryan than thou, motherfucker. Hitler loved him some blue-eyed gingers!"
I stifled a laugh, fearing his Hitler joke crossed the line and might attract negative attention from the other two guards loitering nearby. Shelby ignored the joke and gently followed up on his claim. "Have you seen a lot of alt-right people out here?"
The boisterous ginger looked around. "No, I have not, not since I got out here. There was one Jeep that was circling around, and I was like, 'Why have you passed us multiple times? Are you friend or foe?'" He hocked a loogie and spit in my direction. The large wad of mucus splattered loudly on the pavement a few feet below my camera's microphone.
A shirtless, heavily tattooed man approached the checkpoint. The guy explained in slurred speech how he had been wronged during a fight earlier. He drunkenly appealed his case to the red-bearded security guy, seeking some kind of restitution.
We turned our attention to another border guard. The young man, who was maybe in his mid-20s, rocked anxiously from one foot to the other in front of the passenger door of the Suburban. He wore black, top-of-the-line running shoes. He also sported an extremely expensive-looking black waterproof hiking bag and a windbreaker that probably cost more than many of the people inside the CHAZ had in their bank accounts. A needlepoint belt slung around his chest served as some kind of ill-fitting holster/sling for a giant silver .50 caliber Desert Eagle. The bright chrome of the gun was just barely visible from inside a black holster that had been looped through the belt. The firearm looked cartoonishly large alongside tight-fitting black clothing that clung to his scrawny torso.
Shelby asked him where he got the needlepoint belt, and he said that his mom made it for him, and he thought it worked well. In fact, it did not work well, as the hand cannon swayed from side to side when the young man shifted on his feet. The firearm dangled between 20 degrees and 30 degrees shy of parallel to the ground, which meant it flagged everyone who crossed his flank.
Convinced that the bony boy with the big gun was a hazard to our health, we approached a third guard, an Asian fellow who had a walkie-talkie with a large, silver antenna sticking up near his face. His arms rested on a repurposed plastic road divider. Large white stickers on the barrier read "Defund SPD" in black letters with the symbol of a raised, closed fist--synonymous with the BLM movement and also closely resembling the logo for Maoist China's communist uprising. He looked in our direction as we approached, holding a cigarette in his hand like a writer holds a pen. This time, Shelby launched into her most pressing question right off the bat, "So, is there anyone like running this? Or is it just like a whole group? Because I've seen news reports that claim there's a certain person [Raz] running it?"
The guard clasped his free hand on the butt of the unlit cigarette and pondered the question before he replied, "I can really say with a lot of certainty that there is not a specific person running it."
Shelby waved her hand, indicating that it was time to go. We thanked the guards and walked back toward the police station. Save for the faint sound of a few partiers, things were quiet. We reconvened with Jorge and updated him on the tenuous security situation at the border. After our first night, it was apparent that this no-cop utopia had already devolved to infighting.

* * *
**June 14, 2020: Heart of the CHAZ, Seattle, Washington**
"Which is better--to have laws and agree, or to hunt and kill?"
--Piggy, _Lord of the Flies_
I recorded a portly black man who carried an AR pistol. His girthy figure was clothed in a gray camo sweatshirt with the hood cinched down to his eyes and a bandana pulled up over his face. Affixed to the weapon was a drum magazine capable of holding at least 60 rounds, but it was hard to tell with the circular mag resting on his rotund gut while he stood casually outside the police station.
As I published the scene on Twitter, I heard some commotion around the corner. I scurried to the northeast side of the precinct and found Shelby recording a fight. She employed her signature move, where she holds the phone vertically along the straps of her backpack while recording to appear less obvious.
The fight started after someone slipped inside the perimeter fence surrounding the station and added graffiti to the already copiously spray-painted walls. Another individual intervened by publicly chastising the vandal. As the face-off escalated to physical contact, a few observers entered damage control mode, scanning for anyone who might be recording or broadcasting the mayhem. I was nervous for Shelby, but as the censorship brigade turned their attention to an individual with a larger, more eye-catching camera, she slyly holstered her phone.
Amid the ensuing brouhaha, distant but more frenzied screams reverberated off the boarded-up businesses northeast of our position. Jorge was the first one to hear the turbid echoes and take off. Shelby and I were quick to follow.
We raced a few blocks outside the border of CHAZ, where a pack of two dozen people howled while they smashed a chain-link fence surrounding an auto shop. As I neared the fence, I could see a smaller group inside with cameras and lights aimed in the direction of two men, one of whom was armed with a rifle. They stood next to an F-250 pickup truck with the doors flung open and hazard lights flashing orange on their pale skin. I recognized Jorge's crooked helmet bobbing among the handful of people filming inside the fence.
I ran 10 yards down the sidewalk where the fence was shaking less violently, and I quickly scaled over. Jumping barriers had been a valued skill ever since I successfully evaded police by vaulting a picket fence at age 17--though my less agile buddy got caught, and I had to turn myself in the next day.
The older and taller of the two shop defenders had an AR-15 rifle slung over the front of his body, this one with a standard 30-round magazine. A tactical strap supported the weapon in front of his vest, which held extra mags, as well as his cell phone camera aimed back at the surrounding streamers. His left hand clutched the stock of the rifle while his right hand rested on its handguard. He yelled at me as I approached his periphery for an angle that showed both the mob and the men standing their ground. "Get back! Don't try to get behind me. I don't want anyone behind me." Given that he was heavily armed and this appeared to be his property, I politely acquiesced to his demand.
Turning his attention from me, the rifleman removed his hand from its hold on the rail and waved the gaggle of recorders away. The cries from the other side of the fence calmed down as he stood tall and returned his free hand to the gun.
This scene initially kicked off after someone allegedly broke into their shop and attempted to light a fire. After the two men caught the intruder, they held him at gunpoint and waited for police to arrive. However, the mob showed up at their gate long before the cops, demanding the alleged arsonist's release. The men decided to give in to the crowd rather than wait God knows how long for the officers of the law. They were greatly outnumbered and had no other choice. It was difficult to imagine what would have happened if the man with the rifle had not kept everyone at bay.
On June 20, a 19-year-old man named Horace Lorenzo Anderson was shot four times by another 19-year-old man who worked as a volunteer for the "security" team tasked with defending the citizen-run autonomous zone. When police showed up to investigate the shooting, they were confronted by a violent protest. As if the first homicide and a series of shootings were not enough, it took one more death before the government decided the CHAZistani experiment needed to be shut down.
On June 29, a 16-year-old boy was shot and killed at one of the checkpoints, the same checkpoint where we saw multiple armed men who claimed they were defenders of the postmodern paradise. The car the deceased young man drove was riddled with bullets. According to witnesses, all of whom were sympathetic to the protest, the boy attempted a drive-by shooting before he was gunned down. We will never know what actually happened because the incident was not captured on video, and no impartial witnesses were present. No one was prosecuted for the homicide.
After the shootings, the city simply disbanded the experiment and moved on as if nothing had happened. Progressive city leaders pointed their fingers to the other side of the political aisle while homelessness skyrocketed, and small businesses were destroyed by draconian lockdowns in their own backyards.
On the ride home, we were once again afforded an empty fuselage. We spread our tired bodies out across entire rows, and I should have comfortably drifted to sleep. But I could not get the face of the primal beast that lies within all of us out of my mind.
The local leaders who capitulated the police precinct and the CHAZistanis who conquered it shared a delusion that--due to exponential technological advancement--modern man had somehow evolved beyond our primordial roots. Yet the miracle of wireless internet also tethered us to a window into the world we can't have: from power to money and lust and most of all the fear of a virus that had shattered the world we knew. The electrification of our existence has only left us more prideful, close-minded, envious of some, and hateful towards the rest.
Even with a galvanized effort led by "science," politicians were unable to stop the spread of the invisible killer that was the focus of every news show for over a year. The idealistic armed CHAZ border guards and their proponents in elite media were powerless to pacify their darkest desires to seize control. Within weeks, the cop-free zone turned into a deadly shooting gallery. As in any power vacuum since the dawn of mankind, those who had weapons prevailed over even the most passionate calls for de-escalation.
In _Lord of the Flies_ , the boys were only rescued because a passing warship saw the fire that scorched the whole island. When the adults showed up, a British naval officer inquired, "Nobody killed, I hope? Any dead bodies?"
"Only two. And they've gone," Ralph replied.
